preserveUserBrands: assets/brands/* except assets/brands/example/ (example is updatable, user brands are not)

featureFlags._doc: Runtime feature flags read at Electron startup. Do NOT rely on these for security-critical defaults — a stale version.json on an offline client stays at the shipped values. Security-critical changes must land as code pushes with a version bump.

notes: v1.21.12: Image preview + Meta push diagnostics + button-click hit-area — three urgent fixes on top of v1.21.11. (1) Image preview was showing the bare reference photo overlaid on top of the AI-generated scene whenever the user's brand used an EDIT model (nano-banana-pro/edit, nano-banana-2/edit, any */edit slug). The composite mode was running unconditionally — for edit models, the AI had ALREADY incorporated the reference image into the scene, so overlaying the cutout again produced a flat sticker on top of a perfectly good model shot. The same bad path flowed into adImagePath, so users were publishing the wrong creative to Meta and paying for impressions on broken ads. Fix: skip the runtime composite overlay when isEditModel — the model contract is already satisfied by the edit input. Pinned by composite_edit_model_test.go (source-scan + isFalEditModel canonical-slug coverage). (2) Meta push errors were showing only `Object cannot be loaded` with no indication of WHICH resource was missing — Meta's API returns error_user_title + error_user_msg fields specifically for this case (`Pixel not found: 1234567890`, `Page X not found in this ad account`, etc.) but the binary was dropping them at three sites: metaErrorPreview, metaPost non-200 branch, and the batch sub-response handler used by ExecuteBatch. Users were guessing between Page / Pixel / Ad Account / Custom Audience while ads were down. Fix: surface error_user_title + error_user_msg verbatim at all three sites, plus append `— reconnect Meta from the ✦ Magic panel to refresh this asset` when the user_msg mentions a re-OAuth-fixable resource (page, pixel, ad account). 6 new tests in meta_error_preview_test.go cover pixel/page/audience surfacing + the no-misleading-hint asymmetry on custom audiences. (3) Gallery viewer's four action buttons (★ keep / ✗ reject / 🗑 trash / ✕ close) only registered clicks on their inner glyph text node, not the surrounding 36×36 button bbox. Root cause: .gv-toolbar { pointer-events:none } combined with .gv-toolbar > * { pointer-events:auto } only restored events on the DIRECT children (.gv-actions container) — under Electron WebKit the buttons themselves stayed unreachable for clicks outside the glyph. Fix: switch to descendant selector (.gv-toolbar *) so every nested control is reachable. Pinned by gallery-viewer-buttons.test.js (positive + negative selector guards). Bundles tonight's earlier fixes (Wave F NC-ROAS+LTV, currency, Slack tool, international brands; Shopify App-Store install handoff). Tests: 218s Go suite green, 1009 JS green.
whatsNew: Image preview shows the actual generated image — not the reference photo overlaid on top. When using an edit model (the default for brands with a brand-manifest), the AI already places your design in the scene; the previous build then pasted the bare reference cutout on top, breaking both the chat preview AND the creative pushed to Meta. Now the composite step is skipped when the edit model has already incorporated the reference. Existing reference-on-scene composite path stays for text-to-image models (Flux, Seedream, Ideogram).

Meta push errors now tell you which asset is missing. Previously a NOT_FOUND on a stale Pixel / Page / Ad Account / Custom Audience returned an opaque `Object cannot be loaded` — Meta's own `error_user_msg` was being dropped by the binary's error parser. Now you see the exact resource name (`Pixel not found: 1234567890`) plus a one-click reconnect hint when it's a re-OAuth-fixable asset (Page / Pixel / Ad Account). The fix benefits every push path: single-creative push, multi-creative batch push, and bulk upload all share the same error parser.

Gallery viewer action buttons (★ keep / ✗ reject / 🗑 trash / ✕ close) are now clickable across the full button area, not just the icon. Pre-fix only the glyph itself accepted clicks; the surrounding 36×36 button bbox was inert because of a stale CSS direct-child selector chain. Pinned by a regression test so a future selector-tightening sweep can't bring it back.

Connect Shopify in one click from the Shopify App Store — no more typing your store URL in a modal. The new install flow is App Store-compliant (you click 'Add app' on apps.shopify.com → approve scopes → land back in Merlin Desktop with your store connected). Returning merchants who reconnect after a long gap no longer get stuck in the 'install loop' some hit on the previous flow.

Dashboard now shows NC-ROAS (revenue from first-time buyers ÷ ad spend) plus LTV, LTV:CAC, and payback in months — with a confidence label so a brand without enough cohort history sees 'no signal yet' instead of a fake $0. NC-ROAS uses Shopify's authoritative orders_count==1 field — not heuristics — so the number you see is the number Shopify says.

Plus from v1.21.11: currency-aware spending (EUR/GBP/AUD brands), international brand-name slugification (Lalí Cosmetics et al), `mcp__merlin__slack` for Monday-digest hand-off, faster chat scroll + voice transcription, and the Meta intent-tool spend-approval bypass closed.
